LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT UNION CARBIDE CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT CONTINUED FROM MAR-28-2008 TO LAW AND MOTION AT APR-14-2008, 9:30 AM IN DEPT. 302. NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING: CONTINUED TO APRIL 14, 2008. THE COURT REQUIRES THAT THE MOVING PARTY IS TO PROVIDE THE COURT WITH A SET OF COURTESY COPIES OF THEIR PAPERS. WHEN SUBMITTING THE DUPLICATE COPIES, PLEASE PROVIDE A COVER LETTER TO THE COURT REFLECTING THE NEW HEARING DATE SO THAT THE COURTESY COPIES WON'T BE MISPLACED WHEN RECEIVED. JUDGE: CURTIS KARNOW, REPORTER: CARLITO MANDIA, CSR 	689

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT UNION CARBIDE CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. RULING: COUNSEL HAVING SUBMITTED TO THE TENTATIVE RULING, COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING: DENIED, FAILED TO SHIFT THE BURDEN OF PROOF. DEFENDANT'S UNDISPUTED FACTS TEN AND ELEVEN ARE NOT SUPPORTED BY ADMISSIBLE OR PROBATIVE EVIDENCE. PLAINTIFF'S OBJECTIONS TO MEYER DECLARATION 3-25-03 ARE OVERRULED AND TO THE MEYER DECLARATION 7-29-05 ARE SUSTAINED. PLAINTIFF'S OBJECTION TO HAMILTON INTERROGATORY RESPONSES IS SUSTAINED. DEFENDANT SHIFTED THE BURDEN OF PROOF ON THE ISSUES OF FALSE REPRESENTATION AND PUNITIVE DAMAGES AND PLAINTIFF HAS NOT PRESENTED EVIDENCE OF TRIABLE ISSUES OF FACT. SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS GRANTED ON THOSE CLAIMS AND DENIED AS TO THE OTHERS FOR FAILURE TO SHIFT THE BURDEN OF PROOF. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. (302/PJM) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Apr-18-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT UNION CARBIDE CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT.
APPEARANCES: JOHN GOLDSTEIN, EQUIRE (BRAYTON PURCELL, 415.898.1555) APPEARING FOR PLAINTIFF. JOSETTE D. JOHNSON (BRYDON HUGO & PARKER, (415.808.0300) APPEARING FOR DEFENDANT.
RULING: COUNSEL HAVING SUBMITTED TO THE TENTATIVE RULING, COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING: DENIED, FAILED TO SHIFT THE BURDEN OF PROOF. DEFENDANT'S UNDISPUTED FACTS TEN AND ELEVEN ARE NOT SUPPORTED BY ADMISSIBLE OR PROBATIVE EVIDENCE. PLAINTIFF'S OBJECTIONS TO MEYER DECLARATION 3-25-03 ARE OVERRULED AND TO THE MEYER DECLARATION 7-29-05 ARE SUSTAINED. PLAINTIFF'S OBJECTION TO HAMILTON INTERROGATORY RESPONSES IS SUSTAINED. DEFENDANT SHIFTED THE BURDEN OF PROOF ON THE ISSUES OF FALSE REPRESENTATION AND PUNITIVE DAMAGES AND PLAINTIFF HAS NOT PRESENTED EVIDENCE OF TRIABLE ISSUES OF FACT. SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS GRANTED ON THOSE CLAIMS AND DENIED AS TO THE OTHERS FOR FAILURE TO SHIFT THE BURDEN OF PROOF. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. (302/PJM/VC) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600
